# Vibe Test — Code-Aware Browser Testing Agent

> An MCP server and CLI tool for AI-powered browser testing. Designed for vibe coding workflows with Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, and other LLM-powered code editors.

## What is Vibe Test?

Vibe Test is a browser testing agent that reads your codebase, understands every route/form/component, opens a real Playwright browser, explores every interactive element, and reports what works and what breaks — with screenshots.

It works as an **MCP (Model Context Protocol) server** that gives your code editor 11 testing tools, or as a standalone CLI.

## When to use Vibe Test

- You are vibe coding and want to test your app without writing test cases manually
- You want an AI agent to explore your web app and find bugs
- You need automated browser testing that understands your code
- You want test coverage gap analysis with auto-generated test scenarios
- You need screenshot-based visual verification during development
- You want to test login flows, CRUD operations, forms, and navigation automatically

## Installation

### One-command setup for all editors

```bash
cd /path/to/your/project
npx vibe-test@latest init
```

This single command auto-detects installed editors (Cursor, Claude Code, VS Code, Windsurf, Roo Code) and configures all of them. It creates:
- MCP server configs for each detected editor
- Cursor rules / CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md with tool instructions
- VIBE.md template for project-specific test guidance
- vibe.config.json with sensible defaults

Supported editors: Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, VS Code (Copilot), Roo Code, Cline, Codex, Devin, Zed

### Options

```bash
npx vibe-test init                  # auto-detect, project-level
npx vibe-test init --global         # also register globally
npx vibe-test init --editor cursor  # only configure Cursor
```

### CLI

```bash
npx vibe-test run https://your-app.com --codebase /path/to/project
npx vibe-test converge https://your-app.com --codebase /path/to/project
```

`converge` runs baseline tests then automatically runs follow-up rounds from coverage gaps and failures until pass-rate / gap thresholds or max rounds.

## MCP Tools (11 tools)

1. `scan_codebase` — Analyze project structure, routes, forms, existing tests, coverage gaps
2. `login` — Authenticate in a real browser, capture tokens and cookies
3. `scan_page_elements` — Discover all interactive elements on any page
4. `explore_page` — Click every button, fill every input, test every link
5. `execute_scenario` — Run a specific multi-step test scenario
6. `get_coverage` — View test coverage map and gap analysis
7. `suggest_tests` — Get prioritized, executable test scenarios from gap analysis
8. `take_screenshot` — Visual verification of any page
9. `generate_report` — HTML report with screenshots (auto-opens in browser)
10. `run_full_test` — All-in-one: scan → execute → explore → report
11. `cleanup` — Close browsers and reset session

## Key Features

- **Code-aware**: Reads your source code to understand routes, forms, components, and existing tests
- **Framework detection**: Supports React, Next.js, Vue, Angular, Svelte, and more
- **Self-improving**: Learns working selectors, route timings, and failure patterns across runs
- **No LLM dependency**: Uses heuristic verification — the editor LLM is the brain
- **Screenshot reports**: Interactive HTML reports with step-level screenshots
- **Action blocklist**: Prevents destructive actions (delete account, cancel subscription)
- **Persistent auth**: Saves login credentials and reuses sessions across runs

## Technology

- TypeScript, Node.js
- Playwright for browser automation
- Model Context Protocol (MCP) SDK for editor integration
- JSON-RPC over stdio for communication

## Source

GitHub: https://github.com/AishwaryShrivastav/vibe-testing
npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/vibe-test
